name: Sorrel Pesto for Pasta
recipeIngredient: 2 cups coarsely chopped fresh sorrel (ribs removed)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor or blender, puree the sorrel, parsley, garlic, Parmesan, and pine nuts.
2. Gradually add the olive oil while the processor is running.
3. Transfer the pesto to a jar with a tight-fitting lid.
4. Chill the pesto, covered, for up to 2 weeks.
To use the pesto:
1. For every pound of dried pasta cooking in boiling water, stir together in a heated serving bowl 3/4 cup of the pesto and 2/3 cup of the hot cooking water.
2. When the pasta is al dente, drain it in a colander.
3. Add the drained pasta to the pesto mixture.
4. Toss the mixture until the pasta is coated well. Vermicelli works very well with this recipe.
